text: Beta-glycine (β-glycine), chemical formula C2H5NO2, molecular weight 75.07, is a single-component organic molecular crystal and a metastable polymorph of glycine. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar monoclinic space group P2_1 (No. 4), with unit cell a = 5.0757 Å, b = 6.1737 Å, c = 5.3882 Å and β = 113.422°, Z = 2. The structure is built from hydrogen-bonded glycine zwitterions. Beta-glycine is piezoelectric and is known for a giant shear piezoelectric coefficient (d16) of about 178 pm/V. In this work, dual-AC-resonance-tracking piezoresponse force microscopy (PFM) measured an effective piezoelectric coefficient of about 9.9 pm/V for the beta-glycine crystal and 10.3 pm/V for a flexible beta-glycine/polycaprolactone (PCL) composite film, and ferroelectric hysteresis-loop measurements demonstrated ferroelectric switching. The composite film was used to build a biocompatible piezoelectric nanogenerator (PENG) that produced electrical output under mechanical pressure and ultrasound. The crystal structure (Crystallography Open Database entry 7128793) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 100 K with an R factor of 2.29%, a density of 1.609 g/cm3, a colorless needle habit, and was reported by Hoser et al. in Chemical Communications (2021). The piezoelectric and nanogenerator characterization was reported by Bai et al. in Nano Energy (2026).
property_ref_title: Antisolvent-induced multiscale β-glycine for preparing flexible piezoelectric films
